scoring_notes: Rows define hidden check intent, public setup/assertion contract, and fairness constraints. Convert each row to executable black-box test_code before candidate runs.

items.description: Validates job construction defaults and JSON-compatible args.
items.cross_feature_contract: Single job records expose public defaults without touching queue state.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Rejects invalid queue, kind, priority, max_attempts, and non-object args atomically.
items.cross_feature_contract: Invalid job input never creates visible job rows.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Allows only valid state transitions from available/scheduled/executing/retryable to terminal states.
items.cross_feature_contract: Transition primitive preserves lifecycle contract before system rows compose it.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Computes due availability from explicit virtual time for scheduled jobs.
items.cross_feature_contract: Due-time primitive agrees with virtual clock semantics.
items.feature_pure_notes: Feature-pure: scheduler plus direct job fixture.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Orders claim candidates by priority, scheduled/created time, then stable ID.
items.cross_feature_contract: Claim ordering primitive is deterministic before queue integration.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Computes deterministic retry delay for attempts 1, 2, and 3.
items.cross_feature_contract: Retry schedule uses public backoff formula.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Marks attempt exhaustion as discarded rather than retryable.
items.cross_feature_contract: Terminal retry primitive prevents later queue drift.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Builds uniqueness keys from kind, queue, and args field subsets.
items.cross_feature_contract: Uniqueness primitive uses public fields only.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Expires uniqueness windows by explicit virtual time.
items.cross_feature_contract: Expired windows stop blocking new jobs consistently.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Applies reject and replace conflict decisions without queue setup.
items.cross_feature_contract: Conflict primitive returns public outcome independent of storage.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Computes next cron slots from every_seconds and virtual UTC time.
items.cross_feature_contract: Cron slot primitive is deterministic.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Rejects invalid cron names, intervals, and payloads atomically.
items.cross_feature_contract: Invalid cron config never creates schedule state.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Appends ordered public lifecycle events with stable sequence fields.
items.cross_feature_contract: Event stream primitive has deterministic ordering.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Rejects malformed or impossible lifecycle events.
items.cross_feature_contract: Malformed events cannot poison metrics/replay.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Aggregates state, queue, kind, success, failure, discard, cancel, and retry counts from event records.
items.cross_feature_contract: Metrics primitive maps public events to public rollups.
items.feature_pure_notes: Feature-pure: direct event records, no queue API.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Persists and reloads a direct job row fixture without changing public fields.
items.cross_feature_contract: Store primitive round-trips public records.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Rejects duplicate stable job IDs atomically.
items.cross_feature_contract: Storage primitive cannot create identity drift.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Serializes jobs, events, metrics, cron, uniqueness, and recovery records into JSON-compatible export data.
items.cross_feature_contract: Export primitive includes every public ledger.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Rejects malformed import data without replacing existing state.
items.cross_feature_contract: Import failure is atomic.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: Classifies recovery markers as rollback, roll-forward, or already-clean.
items.cross_feature_contract: Recovery primitive exposes public status before system composition.
items.unit_isolation: direct public module fixture or explicit mock; no integrated JobLedger setup

items.description: API enqueue and CLI jobs report expose the same immediate job.
items.cross_feature_contract: API and CLI are two projections of the same job row.

items.description: CLI enqueue and API jobs report expose the same scheduled job.
items.cross_feature_contract: Scheduled state is consistent across public boundaries.

items.description: API claim updates CLI queue-report and event stream.
items.cross_feature_contract: Claim changes queue projection and events together.

items.description: Completing a job updates history, metrics, queue-report, and events.
items.cross_feature_contract: Terminal success is visible in every projection.

items.description: Failing a job schedules retry and updates attempts, queue visibility, metrics, and events.
items.cross_feature_contract: Retry projection and event/metric projection agree.
items.feature_pure_notes: Integration: crosses retry, queue, metrics, events.

items.description: Max-attempt failure discards job and removes it from claimable queue while retaining history.
items.cross_feature_contract: Discard terminal state agrees with queue and history.

items.description: Cancelling scheduled and executing jobs updates conflict windows, metrics, and events.
items.cross_feature_contract: Cancel semantics are consistent across ledgers.

items.description: Reject uniqueness conflict appears in conflict-report and does not mutate queue counts.
items.cross_feature_contract: Conflict report and queue projection agree.

items.description: Replace uniqueness conflict cancels/replaces older job and records events for both jobs.
items.cross_feature_contract: Replace policy changes rows and events coherently.

items.description: cron-set plus tick creates one scheduled-slot job visible to API and CLI.
items.cross_feature_contract: Cron ledger and job rows agree across boundaries.

items.description: Reopening JobLedger preserves jobs, events, metrics, cron entries, and uniqueness windows.
items.cross_feature_contract: Durable ledgers survive process boundary.

items.description: Exporting and importing preserves job history, queue-report, metrics, and events.
items.cross_feature_contract: Import/export replay keeps projections equivalent.

items.description: Unknown job completion fails without changing metrics, events, or queue counts.
items.cross_feature_contract: Public error atomicity holds across projections.

items.description: Claim order is stable across API claim and CLI claim on identical ledgers.
items.cross_feature_contract: Ordering semantics match across public boundaries.

items.description: Recover command reports clean state after normal operations and does not mutate reports.
items.cross_feature_contract: Recovery no-op keeps projections stable.

items.description: Enqueue, claim, complete, export, import, and report success workflow remains coherent.
items.cross_feature_contract: Rows, events, metrics, queue report, and imported state agree after full success workflow.
items.feature_pure_notes: System: cross-feature workflow; ignore primitive constructor errors.
items.setup_sequence: Create temp ledger; enqueue job through API; claim and complete; export; import into new ledger; read jobs/history/queue-report/metrics/events.
items.public_assertions: Completed job is terminal and retained; queue has no claimable work; events include enqueue/claim/complete; metrics success count is 1 before and after import.
items.cascade_roots_to_ignore: job model validation; CLI parser; export JSON syntax
items.oracle_evidence: Oban retained jobs, lifecycle completion, telemetry; benchmark export/import variant.

items.description: Failure and retry workflow across virtual time keeps attempts, retry schedule, queue visibility, metrics, and events coherent.
items.cross_feature_contract: Retry ledger, queue report, and event stream agree after time advances.
items.setup_sequence: Enqueue max_attempts=3 job; claim; fail at T1; advance virtual time to retry; claim again.
items.public_assertions: Attempt ledger records failure; retryable job becomes claimable only after scheduled_at; metrics failure and retry totals match events.
items.cascade_roots_to_ignore: retry delay primitive; virtual clock parsing
items.oracle_evidence: Oban retry behavior and benchmark deterministic backoff.

items.description: Repeated failures exhaust attempts and discard without losing retained history or metrics.
items.cross_feature_contract: Terminal discard, history, events, and metrics agree.
items.setup_sequence: Enqueue max_attempts=2 job; claim/fail twice across retry schedule.
items.public_assertions: Job is discarded, not claimable; history retains both attempts; metrics discard/failure counts match event stream.

items.description: Cron tick before and after restart/import inserts at most one job per slot.
items.cross_feature_contract: Cron ledger, job rows, events, and metrics agree across replay.
items.setup_sequence: Configure cron every 60s; tick at T; reopen or import/export; tick same T and T+60.
items.public_assertions: Exactly one job per schedule slot; cron ledger records slots; events/metrics reflect inserted jobs without duplicates.
items.oracle_evidence: Oban periodic jobs no duplicate claim; benchmark deterministic cron ledger.

items.description: Reject uniqueness policy prevents duplicate work while preserving conflict report, events, and queue counts.
items.cross_feature_contract: Conflict outcome is visible without queue drift.
items.setup_sequence: Create uniqueness reject policy; enqueue job; enqueue duplicate within window; inspect conflict-report, queue-report, events.
items.public_assertions: Duplicate is rejected; queue counts unchanged; conflict report names blocker; event stream records conflict without phantom job.
items.oracle_evidence: Oban unique jobs; benchmark conflict report.

items.description: Replace uniqueness policy cancels old work, inserts replacement, and keeps history/conflict/events coherent.
items.cross_feature_contract: Replacement affects all ledgers consistently.
items.setup_sequence: Create uniqueness replace policy; enqueue job; enqueue duplicate replacement; inspect jobs/history/events/conflicts.
items.public_assertions: Old job is cancelled or superseded per PRD; new job is claimable; events explain both; conflict report is empty or resolved.
items.oracle_evidence: Oban unique jobs inspiration; benchmark replace variant.

items.description: Two queues with different priorities and scheduled jobs claim independently while global metrics remain coherent.
items.cross_feature_contract: Queue isolation and global rollups agree.
items.setup_sequence: Enqueue jobs across two queues with priorities/schedules; claim from each queue; read global metrics.
items.public_assertions: Queue isolation holds; each queue claims its own eligible jobs; global metrics equal sum across queue events.

items.description: Mixed API and CLI operations produce identical final jobs, queue-report, metrics, and events.
items.cross_feature_contract: Public boundaries share one durable truth.
items.setup_sequence: Perform mixed enqueue/claim/fail/complete via both API and CLI on same ledger; read reports through both surfaces.
items.public_assertions: API and CLI reports are semantically identical for jobs, queue-report, metrics, and events.
items.cascade_roots_to_ignore: CLI JSON formatting only if public

items.description: Simulated interrupted enqueue is recovered without drift among job rows, events, metrics, and recovery report.
items.cross_feature_contract: Recovery restores all projections to one coherent outcome.
items.setup_sequence: Use test hook or prepared recovery marker for interrupted enqueue after row/event partial write; call recover; inspect reports.
items.public_assertions: Recovery rolls forward or back; no drift among job rows, events, metrics, queue-report, and recovery report.
items.oracle_evidence: Oban reliability/transactional control inspiration; benchmark recovery markers.

items.description: Simulated interrupted completion is rolled forward or back coherently across terminal state, events, and metrics.
items.cross_feature_contract: Completion recovery cannot leave terminal/report drift.
items.setup_sequence: Use test hook or prepared marker for interrupted complete; call recover; inspect terminal state/events/metrics.
items.public_assertions: Completion recovery produces one coherent outcome and never double-counts success.

items.description: Export/import after mixed cron, retry, cancel, and complete workflows preserves public behavior.
items.cross_feature_contract: Replay preserves every public projection.
items.setup_sequence: Run mixed cron, retry, cancel, complete workflow; export; import into new ledger; compare public reports.

items.description: Metrics after replay match metrics before replay and event-derived totals.
items.cross_feature_contract: Materialized metrics and event semantics agree.
items.setup_sequence: Run workflow with success/failure/retry/cancel; compare materialized metrics to event-derived metrics before and after import.
items.public_assertions: Metrics are equal to event-derived totals and stable across replay.
items.oracle_evidence: Oban telemetry/metrics inspiration; benchmark event source for metrics.

items.description: Pruning terminal jobs by retention keeps metrics/history summary while removing only eligible job details.
items.cross_feature_contract: Retention affects detail views without corrupting rollups.
items.setup_sequence: Create terminal and non-terminal jobs; advance time; prune with retain_seconds; inspect prune report, jobs, metrics, events.
items.public_assertions: Only old terminal details are pruned; non-terminal jobs remain; metric/event summaries and prune report agree.
items.oracle_evidence: Oban pruner removes final state jobs; benchmark retention variant.

items.description: Invalid complete/fail/cancel sequence in a mixed ledger leaves jobs, events, queue counts, and metrics unchanged.
items.cross_feature_contract: Error atomicity holds across all projections.
items.setup_sequence: Build mixed ledger; attempt invalid complete/fail/cancel sequences; compare reports before/after.
items.public_assertions: Invalid operations fail publicly and leave jobs, events, queue counts, metrics, and conflicts unchanged.

items.description: Repeating the same operation sequence in a fresh ledger yields identical public reports.
items.cross_feature_contract: Deterministic ordering and IDs are stable enough for report equivalence.
items.setup_sequence: Run identical operation sequence in two fresh ledgers with same explicit times; compare public reports.
items.public_assertions: Reports are equivalent for jobs, queue-report, metrics, events, cron, uniqueness, and recovery state.
